executive_summary: Orchestrator coordination PRD for SD-LEO-INFRA-LEO-COMPLETION-001. Solomon checkpoint-3 FINAL ACCEPTANCE (verdict 2ef435e1, 2026-07-20) rejected program-done on 4 grounds (G1-G4) despite 7 foundation SDs shipping real, merged, unit-green code. This SD closes G1-G3 (G4 is a sibling SD dependency) via a shared-substrate-first decomposition into 4 children with a born-fenced dependency DAG: B (manifest desired-state-slot schema + canary isolation harness + U4 threat-model spec, no dependencies) unblocks C (launcher-shell supervisor + kill-supervisor live drill, depends on B) and E (operator cockpit wiring into scripts/fleet-dashboard.cjs + U4 cookie-non-leak proof, depends on B); D (reboot-respawn runner + scheduled task + claude --resume consumer, depends on B and C) closes last. Acceptance for every live-drill claim is a real, non-mocked drill re-run by Solomon on a canary account — the SD's own sourcing pin states nothing is accepted on assertion/unit-only again. This orchestrator delivers no code directly; deliverables live in children B/C/D/E, each carrying its own PRD/handoffs/gates.

functional_requirements.title: Shared substrate: desired-state manifest slots + canary isolation harness + U4 spec (Child B)
functional_requirements.description: Redesign lib/fleet/session-manifest.js from role-count drift math ({role,min}) into DESIRED-STATE SLOTS (name/color/role/account/worktree/model+effort) per the checkpoint-1 REQUIRED amendment, as the frozen source-of-record interface Children C/D/E depend on. Build a canary/isolation harness (dedicated canary account/profile, canary-only callsign namespace, assert-before-kill guard, FLEET_SPAWN_CONTROL_LIVE default-OFF discipline) that today has zero code references. Originate the formal definition of U4 (agent-browser cookie-non-leak guarantee) — a threat model plus an observable proof mechanism — since it is referenced by Solomon's review but undefined anywhere in the codebase.
acceptance_criteria: lib/fleet/session-manifest.js carries slot fields (name/color/role/account/worktree/model/effort) as the respawn source-of-record; computeManifestDrift/normalizeDesiredManifest extended additively, not replaced; naming-collision with sibling SD-LEO-INFRA-FLEET-REGISTRY-MANIFEST-001 explicitly resolved (SSOT decision documented)

Canary isolation harness exists: dedicated canary account/profile, canary-only namespace, assert-before-kill guard (target account_profile===canary), env-gate never set in a live-fleet session — verified by a unit test that a non-canary target is rejected
U4 threat model + proof mechanism documented and reviewed (SECURITY sub-agent pass), ready for Child E to implement against

functional_requirements.title: Launcher shell entrypoint: real killable supervisor + kill-supervisor live drill (Child C, G1a)
functional_requirements.description: Build a real, persistent, killable supervisor process (launcher shell entrypoint) that owns/watches fleet child sessions, extending lib/fleet/spawn-control.js's verb-composition style rather than replacing it. De-mask the existing spawn-control.test.js kill-path test, which today only asserts a mocked spawnFn was called and never exercises the real detached-process behavior.
acceptance_criteria: LIVE DRILL on the Child-B canary account: kill -9 the real supervisor process; all fleet sessions continue uninterrupted
A real (non-mocked) fleet_verb_* event row is emitted by the kill and recorded
spawn-control.test.js's kill-path test exercises the real detached-flag/stdio path, not a mocked spawnFn assertion

functional_requirements.title: Reboot-respawn runner: scheduled task + claude --resume consumer (Child D, G1b/G2)
functional_requirements.description: Build a reboot-respawn runner registered as a Windows Scheduled Task (adapt the reusable buildSchtasksArgs pattern in scripts/setup-eva-watcher-task.mjs) that reads Child B's frozen desired-state-slot manifest and relaunches each slot via `claude --resume` with its captured session UUID, restoring context after a host reboot.
acceptance_criteria: LIVE DRILL: simulate a reboot with zero live session running; the scheduled task fires and each manifest slot relaunches via claude --resume with its captured UUID
A drill record (events/SAER) is persisted proving the respawn occurred
The runner reads slots from Child B's manifest schema, not the retired {role,min} drift math

functional_requirements.title: Operator cockpit wiring: real callers + model/effort chips + relaunch-under-profile U4 proof (Child E, G3/U4)
functional_requirements.description: Wire the already-merged, already-tested lib/fleet/browser-control.js and lib/fleet/session-detail-view.js (confirmed zero callers repo-wide) into scripts/fleet-dashboard.cjs — the concrete, lowest-risk, actively-developed CLI operator surface — as their first real production caller. Render the chairman's named model/effort chips in the same dashboard (data already captured via lib/checkin/steps/model-effort-merge.cjs into claude_sessions.metadata but never rendered). Prove relaunch-under-profile never leaks chairman cookies to the agent browser against Child B's U4 definition.
acceptance_criteria: buildSessionDetailView()/requestBrowserSession()/signalTakeover() are called from scripts/fleet-dashboard.cjs in a real (non-test) code path, grep-provable as a production caller
Model/effort chips render per session in scripts/fleet-dashboard.cjs's WORKERS section, sourced from claude_sessions.metadata
LIVE DRILL: account-switch relaunch-under-profile on the canary account; a U4 spot-check (per Child B's proof mechanism) shows zero chairman cookies visible to the agent browser

functional_requirements.title: Cross-child acceptance discipline: no assertion/unit-only acceptance, ever again
functional_requirements.description: Every live-drill acceptance criterion across Children C/D/E is verified by Solomon's checkpoint-3 re-run on the Child-B canary account, never by a unit test or mock alone — the exact failure mode this SD exists to close (per the SD's own sourcing pin). This orchestrator's own LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL depends on sibling SD-LEO-INFRA-LEADFINAL-ACCEPTANCE-INTEGRITY-001 (in_progress at PLAN_PRD) so that the LEAD-FINAL gate itself cannot silently downgrade a live claim to a unit-test claim.
acceptance_criteria: Every FR-2/FR-3/FR-4 live-drill AC has a drill/event DB record, not just a passing unit test, before its child SD reaches LEAD-FINAL
This orchestrator's LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL is not run until SD-LEO-INFRA-LEADFINAL-ACCEPTANCE-INTEGRITY-001 has shipped (or an explicit chairman-approved exception is recorded)
Solomon checkpoint-3 is re-run against the completed children and upgrades from PARTIAL to ACCEPT

technical_requirements.title: Born-fenced dependency DAG
technical_requirements.description: Enforced via leo-create-sd.js's --depends-on born-fencing (lib/fleet/claim-eligibility.cjs draftDepsSatisfied): B has no deps; C depends on B; D depends on B and C; E depends on B — no child is claimable before its dependencies complete.

technical_requirements.title: Frozen slot-schema interface
technical_requirements.description: Child B's slot schema is a frozen, versioned interface consumed by both D (respawn) and E (relaunch-under-profile) — schema changes after B ships require a coordinated update across both consumers, not silent drift.

technical_requirements.title: Shared canary harness, not duplicated
technical_requirements.description: The canary isolation harness (Child B) is a shared precondition for every live drill in C, D, and E; it is built once in B, not duplicated per child.

technical_requirements.title: Actual-state SSOT untouched
technical_requirements.description: No SD in this decomposition modifies lib/fleet/session-registry.js or lib/fleet/session-registry-adapter.js (the actual-state SSOT) — only lib/fleet/session-manifest.js (desired-state) is redesigned.

test_scenarios.steps: Enable FLEET_SPAWN_CONTROL_LIVE on the canary account; start the launcher shell; kill -9 the supervisor
test_scenarios.expected: Fleet sessions continue uninterrupted; a real fleet_verb_* event is recorded; registry self-heals on restart

test_scenarios.steps: Trigger the scheduled task with zero active fleet sessions
test_scenarios.expected: Each manifest slot relaunches via claude --resume with its captured UUID; drill record persisted

test_scenarios.steps: Open scripts/fleet-dashboard.cjs, attach to a session, drive the agent-browser, switch accounts
test_scenarios.expected: Real production callers reached (grep-provable); model/effort chips render; U4 cookie non-leak holds under relaunch-under-profile

test_scenarios.scenario: Canary isolation guard rejects a non-canary target
test_scenarios.steps: Attempt the kill-drill action against a non-canary account_profile
test_scenarios.expected: The assert-before-kill guard refuses the action; no live fleet session is disrupted

test_scenarios.steps: Run the existing computeManifestDrift/normalizeDesiredManifest unit suite after the slot-schema extension
test_scenarios.expected: All pre-existing role-count drift tests still pass unchanged; new slot-field tests pass

risks.risk: Killing a real process on the canary account could disrupt live fleet work if isolation isn't airtight.
risks.mitigation: Child B builds the canary/isolation harness FIRST (assert-before-kill guard, canary-only namespace, env-gate discipline) as a hard prerequisite before any child runs a live kill/relaunch drill.

risks.risk: U4 (cookie-non-leak) is undefined anywhere in the codebase; Child E cannot write a non-vacuous acceptance test against an undefined guarantee.
risks.mitigation: Child B originates the formal U4 threat model + proof mechanism, reviewed by SECURITY, before Child E's EXEC phase begins.

risks.risk: The manifest schema redesign (Child B) risks silently repurposing sibling SD-LEO-INFRA-FLEET-REGISTRY-MANIFEST-001's concept, given an already-flagged naming collision.
risks.mitigation: Explicit SSOT/ownership decision required at Child B's PLAN phase (extend additively with a regression pin, or split to a distinctly-named module) before EXEC.

risks.risk: This orchestrator's completion depends on sibling SD-LEO-INFRA-LEADFINAL-ACCEPTANCE-INTEGRITY-001, which is still in_progress and owned by a different session.
risks.mitigation: Children B/C/D/E can proceed through LEAD/PLAN/EXEC independently now; only this orchestrator's own LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL is gated on the sibling, tracked explicitly rather than silently blocking all downstream work.

consumers: Solomon (checkpoint-3 live-drill re-run — the sole acceptance authority for this SD's claims)
The fleet coordinator (consumes the launcher-shell supervisor, reboot-respawn runner, and manifest slots for day-to-day fleet operation once shipped)
The chairman (consumes the operator cockpit wiring in scripts/fleet-dashboard.cjs, model/effort chips)
dependencies: lib/fleet/spawn-control.js and lib/fleet/session-manifest.js (already-merged, extended by this decomposition)
lib/fleet/browser-control.js and lib/fleet/session-detail-view.js (already-merged SD-LEO-INFRA-SESSION-VIEW-BROWSER-001 A/B, wired in by Child E)
Sibling SD-LEO-INFRA-LEADFINAL-ACCEPTANCE-INTEGRITY-001 (in_progress) gates this orchestrator's own LEAD-FINAL-APPROVAL
data_contracts: lib/fleet/session-manifest.js desired-state slot schema (name/color/role/account/worktree/model/effort) — the frozen interface Children D and E consume
runtime_config: FLEET_SPAWN_CONTROL_LIVE (existing, default-OFF) — gates every live-drill action across Children C, D, E; canary-scoped only per Child B's isolation harness
observability_rollout: fleet_verb_* coordination events (real, non-mocked) emitted by the kill-supervisor and relaunch-under-profile live drills
Reboot-respawn drill records persisted to the events/SAER trail
Solomon checkpoint-3 re-run verdict (PARTIAL -> ACCEPT) is the rollout's terminal observability signal

system_architecture.summary: Four dependency-DAG'd child SDs extend the existing fleet-control-plane modules (spawn-control.js, session-manifest.js, browser-control.js, session-detail-view.js) with a real supervisor process, a slot-shaped manifest, a reboot-respawn runner, and a wired operator surface.

components.name: lib/fleet/session-manifest.js
components.change: EXTEND — add desired-state slot fields (name/color/role/account/worktree/model/effort) alongside existing role-count drift math

components.name: lib/fleet/spawn-control.js
components.change: EXTEND — add a persistent supervisor process layer on top of the existing spawn/attach/stop/restart verbs

components.name: NEW launcher-shell module
components.change: NEW — the real, killable supervisor process entrypoint (Child C)

components.name: NEW reboot-respawn runner + Scheduled Task registration
components.change: NEW — adapts scripts/setup-eva-watcher-task.mjs's buildSchtasksArgs pattern (Child D)

components.name: NEW canary/isolation harness module
components.change: NEW — dedicated canary account/profile + assert-before-kill guard (Child B)

components.name: scripts/fleet-dashboard.cjs
components.change: EXTEND — wire in browser-control.js/session-detail-view.js as first real callers; render model/effort chips (Child E)

components.name: lib/fleet/browser-control.js, lib/fleet/session-detail-view.js
components.change: NO CHANGE (already merged/tested) — consumed for the first time by Child E

implementation_approach.summary: Shared-substrate-first: build Child B's frozen interfaces and canary harness before any live drill, then fan out C/D/E respecting the dependency DAG.
steps: Child B: redesign the manifest schema, build the canary/isolation harness, originate the U4 spec — unblocks everything downstream.
Child C (parallel-eligible with E once B ships): build the launcher-shell supervisor, de-mask the kill test, run the live kill-drill.
Child D (after B and C): build the reboot-respawn runner + scheduled task, run the live reboot-respawn drill.
Child E (parallel-eligible with C once B ships): wire browser-control.js/session-detail-view.js into fleet-dashboard.cjs, render model/effort chips, prove U4 via live relaunch-under-profile drill.
Orchestrator LEAD-FINAL: gated on all 4 children completing AND on sibling SD-LEO-INFRA-LEADFINAL-ACCEPTANCE-INTEGRITY-001 shipping, then Solomon re-runs checkpoint-3.

smoke_test_steps.instruction: Enable FLEET_SPAWN_CONTROL_LIVE on a canary account; start the launcher shell; kill -9 the supervisor process
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: Fleet sessions continue uninterrupted; a real fleet_verb_* event is recorded; registry self-heals on restart

smoke_test_steps.instruction: Trigger reboot-respawn (runner + scheduled task) with no live session
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: Each slot relaunches via claude --resume with its captured UUID; drill record persisted

smoke_test_steps.instruction: Open scripts/fleet-dashboard.cjs, attach to a session, drive the agent-browser, switch accounts
smoke_test_steps.expected_outcome: Real production callers reached; model/effort chips render; U4 cookie non-leak holds under relaunch-under-profile
